what: 1. Argument names no longer carry a . prefix
title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: Every argument name across public functions dropped leading . — exception .data, stays special pronoun tidyverse collides base::data(). Passing old dotted name now triggers informative error: Rename map — find--replace left column right column scripts: : :
code: # Old (0.2.x) — now errors define_mpi_specs(   "path/to/specs.csv",   .uid            = "uuid",   .poverty_cutoff = 1/3 ) #> Error in `define_mpi_specs()`: #> ! Argument(s) renamed in 0.3.0 in `define_mpi_specs()`: `.uid` -> `uid`. Please update your call. specs <- define_mpi_specs(   "specs.csv",   .uid            = "uuid",   .poverty_cutoffs = c(1/3, 1/2),   .unit_of_analysis = "household" )  result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   .mpi_specs   = specs,   .deprivations = deps,   .by           = region ) specs <- define_mpi_specs(   "specs.csv",   uid             = "uuid",   poverty_cutoffs = c(1/3, 1/2),   unit_of_analysis = "household" )  result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs    = specs,   deprivations = deps,   by           = region )

what: 2. mpi_specs is now required — no more global option
title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: 0.2.x, calling use_global_mpi_specs() stored spec options("mpi_specs") functions picked automatically mpi_specs omitted. implicit lookup gone. : — pass mpi_specs explicitly every time: Omitting mpi_specs now raises error tells exactly :
code: use_global_mpi_specs()                        # stored in options()  dm <- define_deprivation(df, drinking_water, .cutoff = drinking_water == 2)                                               # ↑ silently read from options() specs <- global_mpi_specs()                   # load once …  dm <- define_deprivation(   df, drinking_water,   cutoff    = drinking_water == 2,   mpi_specs = specs                           # … then pass it ) define_deprivation(df_household, drinking_water, cutoff = drinking_water == 2) #> Error in `define_deprivation()`: #> ! No MPI Specs supplied. Pass the result of `define_mpi_specs()` as `mpi_specs`, or use `global_mpi_specs()` to load the built-in Global MPI spec.

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: Replace every call global_mpi_specs() (define_mpi_specs() custom spec file):
code: # Soft-deprecated: raises a warning but still works specs <- use_global_mpi_specs() #> Warning: `use_global_mpi_specs()` was deprecated in mpindex 0.3.0. #> ℹ Please use `global_mpi_specs()` instead. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. # Before use_global_mpi_specs()  # After specs <- global_mpi_specs()

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: Previously persist spec options() via: argument still exists code doesn’t break immediately, now emits deprecation warning effect. Remove calls store return value instead:
code: define_mpi_specs("specs.csv", .save_as_global_options = TRUE) # Before define_mpi_specs("specs.csv", .save_as_global_options = TRUE) # later code called getOption("mpi_specs")  # After specs <- define_mpi_specs("specs.csv") # pass specs explicitly to each function

what: 5. compute_mpi() has a new signature — old profile workflow moved
title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: 0.2.x, compute_mpi() accepted .deprivation_profile list pre-computed deprivation matrices. workflow now separate function compute_mpi_from_profile(). new compute_mpi() uses inline deprived() cutoffs instead: : — Option : new inline API (compute_mpi) — Option B: keep profile workflow (compute_mpi_from_profile)
code: dm1 <- define_deprivation(df, drinking_water, .cutoff = drinking_water == 2, ...) dm2 <- define_deprivation(df, electricity,    .cutoff = electricity == 2,    ...)  result <- compute_mpi(   df,   .mpi_specs          = specs,   .deprivation_profile = list(drinking_water = dm1, electricity = dm2) ) specs  <- global_mpi_specs()  result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs    = specs,   deprivations = list(     drinking_water = deprived(drinking_water == 2),     electricity    = deprived(electricity == 2)   ) ) specs <- global_mpi_specs()  dm1 <- define_deprivation(df_household, drinking_water,                            cutoff = drinking_water == 2, mpi_specs = specs) dm2 <- define_deprivation(df_household, electricity,                            cutoff = electricity == 2,    mpi_specs = specs)  result <- compute_mpi_from_profile(   df_household,   list(drinking_water = dm1, electricity = dm2),   mpi_specs = specs )

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: boolean .collapse = TRUE argument define_deprivation() deprived() replaced collapse_fn, accepts actual aggregation function. : : Common replacements:
code: deprived(attending_school == 2, .collapse = TRUE)      # used any() internally deprived(attending_school == 2, collapse_fn = max)     # explicit function

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: Two arguments removed save_mpi(): new include_deprivation_matrix argument (default TRUE) controls whether deprivation-matrix sheets written Excel file. : :
code: save_mpi(result, .formatted_output = TRUE, .include_table_summary = FALSE) save_mpi(result, mpi_specs = specs, include_deprivation_matrix = TRUE)

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: default separator used constructing variable_name columns inside mpi_specs_df changed ">" "__". affects relied auto-generated variable_name values matching specific pattern. : d01_i01>drinking_water : d01_i01__drinking_water Pass names_separator = ">" explicitly restore old behaviour:
code: specs <- define_mpi_specs("specs.csv", names_separator = ">")

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
text: mpindex 0.3.0 requires R ≥ 4.1.0 (released May 2021). native pipe |> \(x) lambda syntax used throughout package internals. Update R older version.

title: Migrating to mpindex 0.3.0
code: [ ] Strip `.` prefix from every argument name (except `.data`) [ ] Replace use_global_mpi_specs() with specs <- global_mpi_specs() [ ] Remove .save_as_global_options = TRUE; store the return value instead [ ] Pass mpi_specs = specs explicitly in define_deprivation(),     compute_mpi_from_profile(), and save_mpi() [ ] Replace .collapse = TRUE with collapse_fn = max (or collapse_fn = any) [ ] Move deprivation-profile workflows to compute_mpi_from_profile() or     switch to the new inline deprived() API in compute_mpi() [ ] Remove .formatted_output / .include_table_summary from save_mpi() calls [ ] Verify your R version is ≥ 4.1.0

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) measures poverty just income, whether people deprived across multiple areas lives — health, education, living standards. person household considered poor fall short enough areas time. approach used Alkire-Foster (AF) method, developed Sabina Alkire James Foster Oxford Poverty Human Development Initiative (OPHI). basis Global MPI published annually OPHI UNDP. mpindex package makes straightforward compute MPI survey data R.

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub: load package:
code: install.packages("mpindex") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("yng-me/mpindex") library(mpindex)

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: Computing MPI mpindex follows three steps: Define indicators — load specification file describes dimensions, indicators, weights. Specify deprivation cutoffs — tell package household considered deprived indicator. Compute explore results — run compute_mpi() get index, headcount ratio, intensity, indicator contributions. sections walk step using built-dataset.

what: What goes in a specification file?
title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: specification file tells mpindex indicators organized. must contain columns (column names case-sensitive): package accepts .csv, .xlsx, .json, .txt (tab-separated) files.

what: Using the built-in Global MPI specification
title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: mpindex ships Global MPI specification example. global_mpi_specs() (formerly global_mpi_specs() now deprecated) shortcut loads one line: uid argument names column dataset uniquely identifies household (unit analysis). built-dataset, "uuid" (might different). Behind scenes equivalent : Global MPI covers 10 indicators across three dimensions, weighted equally within dimension:
code: mpi_specs <- global_mpi_specs(uid = "uuid", unit_of_analysis = 'households') specs_file <- system.file("extdata", "global-mpi-specs.csv", package = "mpindex") mpi_specs  <- define_mpi_specs(specs_file, uid = "uuid", unit_of_analysis = 'households')

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: computing custom MPI, different sets dimensions indicators, create CSV (Excel) file follows column structure load way: can pass multiple cutoffs want compare results across different poverty thresholds:
code: mpi_specs <- define_mpi_specs(   mpi_specs_file = "path/to/my-specs.csv",   uid = "household_id",   poverty_cutoffs = 1/3 # default: a household needs >= 33% weighted deprivation score to be MPI poor ) mpi_specs <- define_mpi_specs(   "path/to/my-specs.csv",   uid = "household_id",   poverty_cutoffs = c(0.20, 1/3, 0.50)   # 20%, 33%, 50% )

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: package includes synthetic household survey data modelled Global MPI: df_household — one row per household, containing household-level variables. df_household_roster — one row per household member, containing individual-level variables (e.g. nutrition, school attendance). two tables? indicators (e.g. whether child household attending school) come individual-level data must collapsed household level. mpindex handles automatically — just tell table use collapse .

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: compute_mpi() takes household data, specifications, list deprivation cutoffs — one per indicator. cutoff logical expression wrapped deprived() evaluates TRUE household deprived. result named list:
code: mpi_result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = list(      # --- Health ---     nutrition = deprived(       undernourished == 1 & age < 70,   # deprived if any member under 70 is undernourished       .data        = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max                 # household is deprived if any member is deprived     ),     child_mortality = deprived(with_child_died == 1),      # --- Education ---     year_schooling = deprived(       completed_6yrs_schooling == 2,       .data        = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max     ),     school_attendance = deprived(       attending_school == 2 & age %in% 5:24,       .data        = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max     ),      # --- Living Standards ---     cooking_fuel   = deprived(cooking_fuel %in% c(4:6, 9)),     sanitation     = deprived(toilet > 1),     drinking_water = deprived(drinking_water == 2),     electricity    = deprived(electricity == 2),     housing        = deprived(       roof %in% c(5, 7, 9) | walls %in% c(5, 8, 9, 99) == 2 | floor %in% c(5, 6, 9)     ),     assets = deprived(!(       (asset_tv + asset_telephone + asset_mobile_phone + asset_computer +          asset_animal_cart + asset_bicycle + asset_motorcycle +          asset_refrigerator) > 1 &         (asset_car + asset_truck) > 0     ))   ) ) names(mpi_result) #> [1] "index"              "contribution"       "headcount_ratio"    #> [4] "deprivation_matrix"

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: Pass break results grouping variable (e.g. urban vs. rural):
code: compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = list(...),   by = class        # column in df_household )

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: prefer build inspect deprivation indicator combining , use define_deprivation() create one separately, pass compute_mpi_from_profile(): useful data preparation indicators complex want examine intermediate results.
code: dp <- list()  dp$nutrition <- df_household_roster |>   define_deprivation(     indicator = nutrition,     cutoff = undernourished == 1 & age < 70,     collapse_fn = max,     mpi_specs = mpi_specs   )  dp$drinking_water <- df_household |>   define_deprivation(     indicator = drinking_water,     cutoff = drinking_water == 2,     mpi_specs = mpi_specs   )  # ... define all remaining indicators, then: mpi_result <- compute_mpi_from_profile(df_household, dp, mpi_specs = mpi_specs)

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: mpi_result named list four components. component named list keyed poverty cutoff used — e.g. k_33 33% cutoff.

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: three key numbers : H (headcount ratio) — share population multidimensionally poor. (intensity) — among poor, fraction weighted indicators deprived average. MPI — product H × , capturing many people poor poor .
code: mpi_result$index$k_33

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: much indicator drive overall MPI? contribution table answers — values sum 100% across indicators.
code: mpi_result$contribution$k_33

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: uncensored headcount ratio shows raw deprivation rate indicator, regardless whether household crosses poverty line. censored version counts households also identified multidimensionally poor.
code: mpi_result$headcount_ratio$uncensored   # deprivation rate — all households mpi_result$headcount_ratio$k_33         # deprivation rate — poor households only

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: deprivation matrix records household’s individual score indicator flags. first rows: applying poverty cutoff, households deprivation score threshold indicator flags set zero — counted poor: save memory, can exclude deprivation matrices output setting include_deprivation_matrix = FALSE compute_mpi().
code: mpi_result$deprivation_matrix$uncensored |> head() mpi_result$deprivation_matrix$k_33 |> head()

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: save_mpi() writes results formatted Excel workbook: component gets sheet. also include specification table reference:
code: save_mpi(mpi_result, mpi_specs = mpi_specs, filename = "MPI Results") save_mpi(   mpi_result,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   filename = "MPI Results",   include_specs = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: complete workflow one place:
code: library(mpindex)  # 1. Load specifications mpi_specs <- global_mpi_specs(uid = "uuid", unit_of_analysis = 'households')  # 2. Compute the MPI mpi_result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = list(     nutrition = deprived(undernourished == 1 & age < 70, .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),     child_mortality = deprived(with_child_died == 1),     year_schooling = deprived(completed_6yrs_schooling == 2, .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),     school_attendance = deprived(attending_school == 2 & age %in% 5:24, .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),     cooking_fuel = deprived(cooking_fuel %in% c(4:6, 9)),     sanitation = deprived(toilet > 1),     drinking_water = deprived(drinking_water == 2),     electricity = deprived(electricity == 2),     housing = deprived(roof %in% c(5, 7, 9) |walls %in% c(5, 8, 9, 99) == 2 | floor %in% c(5, 6, 9)),     assets = deprived(       !(asset_tv +          asset_telephone +          asset_mobile_phone +          asset_computer +          asset_animal_cart +          asset_bicycle +          asset_motorcycle +          asset_refrigerator) > 1 &        (asset_car + asset_truck) > 0     )   ) )  # 3. Inspect results mpi_result$index$k_33          # headline MPI, H, A mpi_result$contribution$k_33   # indicator contributions (%) mpi_result$headcount_ratio$k_33 mpi_result$deprivation_matrix$k_33  # 4. Save to Excel save_mpi(mpi_result, mpi_specs = mpi_specs, filename = "MPI Results")

title: Getting Started with mpindex
text: OPHI — Apply Alkire-Foster Method Global MPI 2023 Report Package documentation: ?compute_mpi, ?define_mpi_specs, ?define_deprivation, ?save_mpi

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: Household surveys rarely sample households equal probability. Urban areas, minority groups, remote regions may - -sampled improve precision. household therefore assigned sampling weight records many population households represents. compute MPI without accounting weights, every sampled household counts equally — gives biased estimates true population. weights, household weight 3.2 contributes 3.2 times much H, , indicator means household weight 1.0. Surveys also often use complex design — stratified sampling (separate strata urban/rural) combined cluster sampling (selecting geographic clusters, households within ). Ignoring structure leads standard errors small confidence intervals narrow. mpindex supports survey package.

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
code: install.packages("survey")   # only needed once

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: vignette add synthetic survey columns built-dataset. real work columns come survey microdata. Define deprivation cutoffs (main vignette):
code: library(mpindex) library(survey) #> Loading required package: grid #> Loading required package: Matrix #> Loading required package: survival #>  #> Attaching package: 'survey' #> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics': #>  #>     dotchart mpi_specs <- global_mpi_specs(uid = "uuid") set.seed(42) n <- nrow(df_household)  df_hh <- df_household df_hh$hh_weight <- runif(n, 0.8, 2.5)   # sampling weight df_hh$strata    <- sample(c("urban", "rural"), n, replace = TRUE) df_hh$psu       <- sample(1:30, n, replace = TRUE)  # primary sampling unit deprivations <- list(   nutrition         = deprived(undernourished == 1 & age < 70,                                .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),   child_mortality   = deprived(with_child_died == 1),   year_schooling    = deprived(completed_6yrs_schooling == 2,                                .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),   school_attendance = deprived(attending_school == 2 & age %in% 5:24,                                .data = df_household_roster, collapse_fn = max),   cooking_fuel      = deprived(cooking_fuel %in% c(4:6, 9)),   sanitation        = deprived(toilet > 1),   drinking_water    = deprived(drinking_water == 2),   electricity       = deprived(electricity == 2),   housing           = deprived(     roof %in% c(5, 7, 9) | walls %in% c(5, 8, 9, 99) == 2 | floor %in% c(5, 6, 9)   ),   assets = deprived(!(     (asset_tv + asset_telephone + asset_mobile_phone + asset_computer +        asset_animal_cart + asset_bicycle + asset_motorcycle +        asset_refrigerator) > 1 &       (asset_car + asset_truck) > 0   )) )

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: common starting point: weight column stratification clustering. arises households selected unequal probability design explicit strata clusters — example, probability-proportional--size (PPS) sample single stage selection. Pass weight. strata cluster needed. can also request standard errors. cluster structure, variance estimated simple random sampling replacement (survey package default ids = ~1):
code: mpi_simple <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   weight = "hh_weight" )  mpi_simple$index$k_33 #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   number_of_cases headcount_ratio intensity   mpi #>             <int>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> #> 1             198           0.354     0.469 0.166 mpi_simple_inf <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   weight = "hh_weight",   inference = TRUE )  mpi_simple_inf$index$k_33[, c("headcount_ratio", "headcount_ratio_se",                                "mpi", "mpi_se")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   headcount_ratio headcount_ratio_se   mpi mpi_se #>             <dbl>              <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1           0.354             0.0351 0.166 0.0171

what: Option A — pass column names directly
title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: simplest way: tell compute_mpi() columns data frame carry weight, stratum, cluster identifiers. four components output — $index, $contribution, $headcount_ratio, $deprivation_matrix — now reflect population-weighted estimates. finite-population correction can also supplied sampling frame contains stratum sizes:
code: mpi_weighted <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   weight = "hh_weight",   strata = "strata",   cluster = "psu" )  mpi_weighted$index$k_33 #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   number_of_cases headcount_ratio intensity   mpi #>             <int>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> #> 1             198           0.354     0.469 0.166 compute_mpi(df_hh, mpi_specs = mpi_specs, deprivations = deprivations,             weight = "hh_weight", strata = "strata", cluster = "psu",             .fpc    = "stratum_size")

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: already survey::svydesign() object — prefer specify design reuse — pass via survey_design: options produce identical point estimates.
code: svy <- svydesign(   ids     = ~psu,   strata  = ~strata,   weights = ~hh_weight,   nest    = TRUE,         # PSU IDs restart within each stratum   data    = df_hh )  mpi_from_design <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   survey_design = svy )  mpi_from_design$index$k_33 #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   number_of_cases headcount_ratio intensity   mpi #>             <int>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> #> 1             198           0.354     0.469 0.166

what: Adding standard errors and confidence intervals
title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: Set inference = TRUE append design-based standard errors 95% confidence intervals alongside every point estimate. intervals use normal approximation clamped [0, 1]. extra columns follow consistent naming pattern: pattern applies intensity, mpi, every indicator column $headcount_ratio. Change confidence level ci_level:
code: mpi_inference <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   weight = "hh_weight",   strata = "strata",   cluster = "psu",   inference = TRUE )  mpi_inference$index$k_33 #> # A tibble: 1 × 13 #>   number_of_cases headcount_ratio intensity   mpi headcount_ratio_se #>             <int>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>              <dbl> #> 1             198           0.354     0.469 0.166             0.0362 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: headcount_ratio_ci_low <dbl>, #> #   headcount_ratio_ci_high <dbl>, intensity_se <dbl>, intensity_ci_low <dbl>, #> #   intensity_ci_high <dbl>, mpi_se <dbl>, mpi_ci_low <dbl>, mpi_ci_high <dbl> compute_mpi(..., inference = TRUE, ci_level = 0.90)   # 90% CI

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: Combine survey weighting get group-specific weighted estimates. group’s H, , MPI computed using design rows group.
code: mpi_by_class <- compute_mpi(   df_hh,   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   deprivations = deprivations,   weight = "hh_weight",   by = class,   inference = TRUE )  mpi_by_class$index$k_33 #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   class number_of_cases headcount_ratio intensity   mpi headcount_ratio_se #>   <chr>           <dbl>           <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>              <dbl> #> 1 Rural              98           0.430     0.515 0.222             0.0519 #> 2 Urban             100           0.279     0.400 0.112             0.0464 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: headcount_ratio_ci_low <dbl>, #> #   headcount_ratio_ci_high <dbl>, intensity_se <dbl>, intensity_ci_low <dbl>, #> #   intensity_ci_high <dbl>, mpi_se <dbl>, mpi_ci_low <dbl>, mpi_ci_high <dbl>

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: instructive compare weighted unweighted estimates. sampling design informative (.e. selection probability correlated poverty status), differences can substantial.
code: mpi_unweighted <- compute_mpi(df_household, mpi_specs, deprivations)  cat("Unweighted H:", round(mpi_unweighted$index$k_33$headcount_ratio, 4), "\n") #> Unweighted H: 0.3788 cat("Weighted   H:", round(mpi_weighted$index$k_33$headcount_ratio,   4), "\n") #> Weighted   H: 0.3542

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: survey arguments work compute_mpi_from_profile():
code: mpi_result <- compute_mpi_from_profile(   df_hh,   deprivation_profile,        # pre-assembled list from define_deprivation()   mpi_specs = mpi_specs,   weight = "hh_weight",   strata = "strata",   cluster = "psu",   inference = TRUE )

title: Survey-Weighted MPI
text: save_mpi() works identically whether output weighted . SE CI columns, present, written sheets alongside point estimates.
code: save_mpi(mpi_inference, mpi_specs = mpi_specs, filename = "MPI Weighted Results")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bhas Abdulsamad. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdulsamad B (2026). mpindex: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via Alkire-Foster Method. R package version 0.3.0, https://github.com/yng-/mpindex.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mpindex: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via the Alkire-Foster Method},   author = {Bhas Abdulsamad},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/yng-me/mpindex}, }

what: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via the Alkire-Foster Method
title: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via the Alkire-Foster Method
text: Estimate Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) measures household survey microdata using Alkire-Foster dual-cutoff counting method. Load indicator specification CSV, Excel, JSON, plain-text file; compute headcount ratio (H), intensity (), MPI = H × ; disaggregate subgroup; export results formatted Excel report. Complex survey designs — stratification, clustering, probability weights — supported box, optional design-based standard errors confidence intervals. Learn vignette("mpindex") ?compute_mpi. Upgrading 0.2.x? See migration guide full list breaking changes /examples.

title: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via the Alkire-Foster Method
text: install mpindex package CRAN: want get latest development version mpindex, install GitHub. Note may need install devtools.
code: install.packages('mpindex') # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github('yng-me/mpindex')

title: Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) via the Alkire-Foster Method
text: Load package successfully completed installation.
code: library(mpindex)

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
text: primary single-call API computing MPI using Alkire-Foster (AF) counting method. Deprivation cutoffs specified inline using deprived helper, making workflow self-contained readable. step--step workflow using pre-assembled deprivation profile, see compute_mpi_from_profile.

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
code: compute_mpi(   .data,   mpi_specs,   deprivations,   ...,   by = NULL,   include_deprivation_matrix = TRUE,   generate_output = FALSE,   mpi_output_filename = NULL,   include_specs = FALSE,   weight = NULL,   strata = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   fpc = NULL,   survey_design = NULL,   inference = FALSE,   ci_level = 0.95 )

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
text: .data data frame row unit analysis. mpi_specs MPI specifications define_mpi_specs. deprivations named list deprived calls. name must exactly match variable mpi_specs. ... Grouping columns (tidyselect) reserved old-name detection. (Optional) Columns group results , passed tidyselect expression, e.g. c(region, sex). include_deprivation_matrix Whether include deprivation matrices. Default TRUE. generate_output Whether write Excel file side effect. Default FALSE. mpi_output_filename Output filename generate_output = TRUE. include_specs Whether include MPI specification sheet Excel output. weight Name sampling-weight column .data. supplied, estimates survey-weighted. Requires survey package. strata Name stratum column .data. cluster Name cluster / PSU column .data. fpc Name finite-population correction column .data. survey_design pre-built survey::svydesign() object. Provide either weight / strata / cluster / fpc survey_design, . inference Logical. TRUE (survey design supplied), standard errors confidence intervals appended *_se, *_ci_low, *_ci_high columns. Default FALSE. ci_level Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95.

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
text: named list class mpi_output components: $index Named list keyed k_*: MPI, H, , n. $contribution Named list keyed k_*: contribution indicator/dimension. $headcount_ratio Named list uncensored per-k_* censored ratios. $deprivation_matrix Named list uncensored per-k_* matrices.

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
text: Alkire-Foster Method Apply Alkire-Foster Method

title: Compute Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) — compute_mpi
code: specs <- define_mpi_specs(   system.file("extdata", "global-mpi-specs.csv", package = "mpindex"),   uid = "uuid" )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mpi_result <- compute_mpi(   df_household,   mpi_specs = specs,   deprivations = list(     nutrition = deprived(       undernourished == 1 & age < 70,       .data = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max     ),     child_mortality = deprived(with_child_died == 1),     year_schooling = deprived(       completed_6yrs_schooling == 2,       .data = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max     ),     school_attendance = deprived(       attending_school == 2 & age %in% c(5:24),       .data = df_household_roster,       collapse_fn = max     ),     cooking_fuel   = deprived(cooking_fuel %in% c(4:6, 9)),     sanitation     = deprived(toilet > 1),     drinking_water = deprived(drinking_water == 2),     electricity    = deprived(electricity == 2),     housing        = deprived(       roof %in% c(5, 7, 9) | walls %in% c(5, 8, 9, 99) == 2 | floor %in% c(5, 6, 9)     ),     assets = deprived(!(       (asset_tv + asset_telephone + asset_mobile_phone + asset_computer +          asset_animal_cart + asset_bicycle + asset_motorcycle +          asset_refrigerator) > 1 &         (asset_car + asset_truck) > 0     ))   ) ) } # }

what: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
text: Computes Multidimensional Poverty Index using pre-assembled deprivation profile — named list produced calling define_deprivation per indicator. lower-level entry point. single-call workflow inline cutoff expressions, use compute_mpi deprived.

title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
code: compute_mpi_from_profile(   .data,   deprivation_profile,   ...,   mpi_specs = NULL,   include_deprivation_matrix = TRUE,   generate_output = FALSE,   mpi_output_filename = NULL,   include_specs = FALSE,   weight = NULL,   strata = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   fpc = NULL,   survey_design = NULL,   inference = FALSE,   ci_level = 0.95 )

title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
text: .data tidy data frame row unit analysis. deprivation_profile named list data frames produced define_deprivation. Names must exactly match variable column mpi_specs. ... Grouping columns (tidyselect), e.g. region, sex. mpi_specs MPI specifications define_mpi_specs. include_deprivation_matrix Whether include deprivation matrices output. Default TRUE. generate_output Whether write Excel file side effect. Default FALSE. mpi_output_filename Output filename generate_output = TRUE. include_specs Whether include MPI specification sheet Excel output. weight Name sampling-weight column .data. supplied, estimates survey-weighted. Requires survey package. strata Name stratum column .data. cluster Name cluster / PSU column .data. fpc Name finite-population correction column .data. survey_design pre-built survey::svydesign() object. Provide either weight / strata / cluster / fpc survey_design, . inference Logical. TRUE (survey design supplied), standard errors confidence intervals appended *_se, *_ci_low, *_ci_high columns. Default FALSE. ci_level Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95.

title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
text: named list class mpi_output components: $index Named list keyed k_*: MPI, H, , n. $contribution Named list keyed k_*: contribution indicator/dimension. $headcount_ratio Named list uncensored per-k_* censored ratios. $deprivation_matrix Named list uncensored per-k_* matrices.

title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
text: Alkire-Foster Method

title: Compute MPI from a deprivation profile — compute_mpi_from_profile
code: specs <- define_mpi_specs(   system.file("extdata", "global-mpi-specs.csv", package = "mpindex"),   uid = "uuid" )  deprivation_profile <- list()  deprivation_profile$drinking_water <- df_household |>   define_deprivation(     indicator = drinking_water,     cutoff    = drinking_water == 2,     mpi_specs = specs   )  # ... (define remaining indicators) ...  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mpi_result <- compute_mpi_from_profile(   df_household, deprivation_profile, mpi_specs = specs ) } # }

title: Define deprivation cutoffs — define_deprivation
text: Sets deprivation cutoff single indicator.   unit analysis, result 0 (deprived), 1   (deprived), NA (missing). additional weighted column   (indicator value × weight) also computed.

title: Define deprivation cutoffs — define_deprivation
code: define_deprivation(   .data,   indicator,   cutoff,   mpi_specs = NULL,   collapse_fn = NULL,   set_na_equal_to = 0,   ... )

title: Define deprivation cutoffs — define_deprivation
text: .data data frame tibble. indicator Name indicator defined MPI Specs (must exactly match variable column). cutoff logical expression evaluates TRUE deprived units. mpi_specs MPI specifications define_mpi_specs. collapse_fn optional function collapse roster-level data unit--analysis level (e.g. max). NULL (default) means collapsing. set_na_equal_to Coerce NA values 0 (deprived, default) 1 (deprived). ... Reserved; passing old dotted names triggers helpful error.

title: Define deprivation cutoffs — define_deprivation

what: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
title: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
text: Use define MPI dimensions, indicators corresponding   weights using supported file types: .xlsx (Excel),   .json, .csv, .txt (TSV). can also set   poverty cutoff list poverty cutoffs.

title: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
code: define_mpi_specs(   mpi_specs_file = NULL,   indicators = NULL,   poverty_cutoffs = NULL,   unit_of_analysis = NULL,   aggregation = NULL,   uid = NULL,   source_of_data = NULL,   names_separator = "__",   save_as_global_options = FALSE,   ... )

title: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
text: mpi_specs_file Path .xlsx, .json, .csv, .txt (TSV) file. file must contain columns: Dimension, Indicator, Variable, Weight, optionally Description. indicators data frame MPI indicators. Alternative mpi_specs_file prefer define indicators inline. poverty_cutoffs Single value vector poverty cutoffs (k). values must (0, 1]. Default NULL automatically set 1/n, n total number dimensions. unit_of_analysis e.g. "individuals", "households". Default NULL. aggregation Column name defines aggregation level. uid Column name containing unique ID (unit analysis). source_of_data Source data; used output footnotes. names_separator Column separator header hierarchy. save_as_global_options longer effect. ... Reserved forward-compatibility; passing old dotted argument names (e.g. .uid) triggers helpful error.

title: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
text: mpi_specs_df object. Pass directly   mpi_specs argument compute_mpi,   define_deprivation, save_mpi.

title: Define MPI specifications: dimensions, indicators, and weights — define_mpi_specs
code: specs_file <- system.file(   "extdata",   "global-mpi-specs.csv",   package = "mpindex" ) system.file("extdata", package = "mpindex") |> list.files() #> [1] "global-mpi-specs.csv"  "global-mpi-specs.json" "global-mpi-specs.txt"  #> [4] "global-mpi-specs.xlsx"

what: Specify a deprivation cutoff for use in compute_mpi — deprived
title: Specify a deprivation cutoff for use in compute_mpi — deprived
text: helper captures bare deprivation cutoff expression   optional per-indicator settings use inside deprivations   argument compute_mpi.

title: Specify a deprivation cutoff for use in compute_mpi — deprived
code: deprived(.cutoff, .data = NULL, collapse_fn = NULL, set_na_equal_to = 0, ...)

title: Specify a deprivation cutoff for use in compute_mpi — deprived
text: .cutoff bare logical expression evaluated indicator's data frame. Rows evaluates TRUE considered deprived. .data optional data frame use indicator instead primary .data passed compute_mpi. Useful one indicators different unit analysis (e.g. person-level roster). collapse_fn optional function applied collapse roster-level data unit--analysis level (e.g. max flag household deprived member deprived). NULL (default), collapsing performed. NAs removed calling function; values NA result NA. set_na_equal_to Coerce NA deprivation values 0 (deprived, default) 1 (deprived). ... Reserved; passing old dotted names (e.g. .collapse_fn) triggers helpful error.

title: Specify a deprivation cutoff for use in compute_mpi — deprived

9 - construction material electricity Access electricity: 1 - access electricity; 2 - without access electricity cooking_fuel Fuel use cooking: 1 - electricity; 2 - kerosene (gaas); 3 - liquified petroleum gas (LPG); Defaults TRUE. include_specs Whether include MPI specification separate sheet. Defaults FALSE. ... Reserved; passing old dotted names triggers helpful error.

title: mpindex 0.3.0
text: argument names dropped . prefix (except .data). E.g. .mpi_specs → mpi_specs, .weight → weight, .inference → inference. Passing old dotted name now triggers informative error naming renamed argument replacement (ADR-0006). compute_mpi() now uses new deprived() helper inline deprivation cutoffs. previous .deprivation_profile list workflow available via new compute_mpi_from_profile(). define_deprivation() replaces boolean .collapse argument .collapse_fn — pass function (e.g. max) instead TRUE. save_mpi() now delegates Excel formatting tsg package. .formatted_output .include_table_summary arguments removed. new .include_deprivation_matrix argument controls whether deprivation matrix sheets written. Default .names_separator define_mpi_specs() changed ">" "__". .mpi_specs now required define_deprivation(), compute_mpi_from_profile(), save_mpi(). getOption("mpi_specs") default removed — pass result define_mpi_specs() global_mpi_specs() explicitly. use_global_mpi_specs() deprecated. Use global_mpi_specs() instead. .save_as_global_options define_mpi_specs() deprecated longer effect. Minimum R version bumped 4.1.0.

title: mpindex 0.3.0
text: compute_mpi() — single-call API inline deprived() cutoffs argument disaggregation. compute_mpi_from_profile() — lower-level entry point pre-assembled deprivation profiles (replaces old compute_mpi() signature). deprived() — captures bare cutoff expression optional per-indicator settings (collapse_fn, set_na_equal_to; .data unchanged). global_mpi_specs() — loads built-Global MPI specification. Replaces use_global_mpi_specs(). compute_mpi() returns object class mpi_output. Survey-weighted MPI via weight, strata, cluster, fpc pre-built survey::svydesign() object (survey_design) compute_mpi() compute_mpi_from_profile(). survey package soft dependency (Suggests). inference = TRUE appends design-based standard errors confidence intervals *_se, *_ci_low, *_ci_high columns. Confidence level configurable via ci_level (default 0.95).

title: mpindex 0.3.0
text: Internal computation refactored use dplyr::across() throughout, removing deprecated summarise_at() / mutate_at() / rename_all() calls. compute_headcount_ratio_adjusted() correctly preserves ... grouping variables aggregation also supplied (bug fix). tsg::rename_label() replaces manual attr(..., "label") <- assignments column labels.

title: mpindex 0.3.0
text: 51 new unit tests survey-weighted path covering input methods, point-estimate correctness, SE/CI columns, disaggregation, backward-compatibility.

title: mpindex 0.3.0
text: Main vignette rewritten plain-language explanations step--step narrative. New vignette: Survey-Weighted MPI — covers input paths, inference columns, subgroup disaggregation, weighted vs. unweighted comparison.

title: mpindex 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-09 Changed returned value define_mpi_specs list data frame class mpi_specs_df. default, stores global option named mpi_specs can accessed using getOption('mpi_specs'). specifications now stored attributes. Changed default value .unit_of_analysis households NULL. save_mpi now allows unformatted output (tidy format) generating output. Implemented feature include MPI specification output setting .include_specs tot TRUE save_mpi. Deprecated .names_separator argument define_mpi_specs. Enhancement define_deprivation based #17. Added use_global_mpi_specs() wrapper function use Global MPI specification.
